title: Why can't Apple design its own AI chip?
author: P
text:
S

soAsian
Well-known member

v

"Apple is reportedly seeking to acquire AI semiconductor startups to strengthen its cloud server capabilities. The move comes as Apple’s custom M2 Ultra servers struggle to run large AI models like Google’s Gemini, forcing the company to run parts of its upgraded Siri assistant on Nvidia GPUs within Google Cloud"

This sounds like Apple just wants an AI inference chip. Since it’s not for training, why can’t Apple design its own? Amazon and Google did. Even OpenAI teamed up with Broadcom for its own inference chip. Why does Apple need to buy a company instead of designing its own or teaming up with others? Apple surely has enough money to hire talent or partner up.

I’m not in the semis industry, so I’m not knowledgeable enough to know why Apple is shopping for a company instead of designing its own like other big techs. Hopefully someone can enlighten me.

https://www.androidheadlines.com/2026/07/apple-explores-ai-chip-startup-acquisitions-server.html
